Title: Pyrrocidine, a molecular off switch for fumonisin biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABC transporter; multidrug resistance transporter with two nucleotide-binding domains and two transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: 7600 / M-3125 lineage not clearly separated in paper
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumonisin B1; fumonisin B2; fumonisin B3
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for resistance to pyrrocidine B, but deletion did not significantly alter fumonisin production or maize seedling virulence.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced after 1 h exposure to pyrrocidine A and especially pyrrocidine B in liquid culture.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by pyrrocidine A/B exposure.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pyrrocidine B exposure (10–20 µg/mL); pyrrocidine A exposure; GYAM fumonisin-conducive medium
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; targeted gene deletion; complementation; growth curve assays under pyrrocidine B; fumonisin LC-MS profiling; maize seedling virulence assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvABC3 was highly induced by both pyrrocidines. 2. Deletion caused extreme sensitivity to pyrrocidine B, and complementation restored resistance. 3. Deletion did not significantly change FB1/FB2/FB3 production or maize seedling virulence.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fumonisins are toxic mycotoxins associated with phytotoxicity and animal health problems.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of FvABC3 causes severe pyrrocidine B sensitivity but no detectable change in fumonisin production or virulence under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to FgABC3/FGSG_04580 from Fusarium graminearum; likely functions as a pyrrocidine exporter with substrate specificity. Despite adjacency to FvZEAR, its induction was independent of FvZEAR.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indirect value for biocontrol context: pyrrocidine-producing antagonists may suppress F. verticillioides strains lacking effective FvABC3-mediated tolerance.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FvZEAR (adjacent but not required for induction)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in complementation context; no heterologous promoter reported

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Zinc-binding dehydrogenase; alcohol dehydrogenase-related; conserved domain related to zinc-dependent alcohol dehydrogenases and quinone oxidoreductases
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: 7600 / M-3125 lineage not clearly separated in paper
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumonisin B1; fumonisin B2; fumonisin B3
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a strong negative determinant of fumonisin accumulation; deletion dramatically increased FB1, FB2 and FB3 and enhanced virulence on maize seedlings.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most highly induced gene after pyrrocidine A or B exposure; ~log2 FC 12 in both treatments; high FPKM after induction and near-zero in control.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced by pyrrocidine A/B exposure.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pyrrocidine A/B exposure; GYAM fumonisin-conducive medium; maize seedling infection assay on Silver Queen cultivar
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; gene deletion; genetic complementation; LC-MS fumonisin quantification; maize seedling virulence assay; growth assays on pyrrocidine B and GYAM plates
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvZBD1 was the most highly induced pyrrocidine-responsive gene and lies adjacent to the FUM cluster. 2. Deletion caused >30-fold increase in FB1, ~40-fold increase in FB2, and strong increase in FB3. 3. Deletion also enhanced maize seedling virulence and altered colony morphology, supporting a repressive role in fumonisin-associated pathogenicity.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fumonisins are phytotoxic and harmful mycotoxins linked to animal disease; increased fumonisin correlates with stronger seedling disease symptoms.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion increases fumonisin production and virulence; also slightly delays growth under pyrrocidine B but enhances growth uniformity on GYAM.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed as a non-canonical fumonisin-cluster-associated gene with negative impact on fumonisin biosynthesis; not proven to be a direct biosynthetic enzyme. The mechanism may involve metabolic activity, energy production, or precursor diversion.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High relevance for biocontrol and toxigenicity mitigation: induction of FvZBD1 or pyrrocidine treatment may suppress fumonisin contamination.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FUM cluster genes (genomic adjacency/cofunctional association, not direct physical interaction demonstrated)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in complementation context; no heterologous promoter reported

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Zn(2)-Cys(6) / GAL4-like fungal binuclear cluster transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: 7600 / M-3125 lineage not clearly separated in paper
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumonisin B1; fumonisin B2; fumonisin B3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Although strongly induced by pyrrocidines, FvZEAR was not required for FvABC3 induction and its deletion did not alter pyrrocidine B sensitivity; no causal effect on fumonisin metabolism was established.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by pyrrocidine A and especially pyrrocidine B after 1 h exposure.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by pyrrocidine and reported orthologously by zearalenone in related Fusarium species.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; gene deletion; growth assay under pyrrocidine B; expression test of FvABC3 in ΔFvZEAR background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvZEAR was strongly induced by pyrrocidines. 2. Deletion of FvZEAR did not reduce induction of adjacent transporter gene FvABC3. 3. ΔFvZEAR showed no enhanced sensitivity to pyrrocidine B, so FvZEAR is dispensable for the tested metabolic/response phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FvABC3 was tested and not dependent on FvZEAR for induction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: No positive regulatory effect on FvABC3 induction under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable pyrrocidine B sensitivity phenotype on deletion under tested conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested regulatory gene, but evidence argues against a decisive role in pyrrocidine tolerance or fumonisin control in this study. Possible redundancy with FVEG_10488 is discussed.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited based on current data; not supported as an effective target for fumonisin control.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in complementation context; no heterologous promoter reported
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pyrrocidine A; pyrrocidine B; previously reported zearalenone response in orthologs
